heading: Redemption through use of related corporations
label: 304 Redemption through use of related corporations
notes: References in TextSection 2(a) of the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956, referred to in subsec. (b)(3)(D)(ii), is classified to , Banks and Banking.section 1841(a) of Title 12

Amendments2014—Subsec. (b)(3)(D). , struck out “and special rule” after “Definitions” in heading.Pub. L. 113–295, § 221(a)(48)(B)Subsec. (b)(3)(D)(iii). , struck out cl. (iii). Text read as follows: “In the case of a BHC which is formed before 1985, clause (i) of subparagraph (C) shall not apply.”Pub. L. 113–295, § 221(a)(48)(A)2010—Subsec. (b)(5)(B), (C). added subpar. (B) and redesignated former subpar. (B) as (C).Pub. L. 111–2261998—Subsec. (b)(5)(B), (C). , redesignated subpar. (C) as (B) and struck out heading and text of former subpar. (B). Text read as follows: “For purposes of subparagraph (A), the rules of section 1248(d) shall apply except to the extent otherwise provided by the Secretary.”Pub. L. 105–206, § 6010(d)(1)Subsec. (b)(6). , added par. (6).Pub. L. 105–206, § 6010(d)(2)1997—Subsec. (a)(1). , amended last sentence generally. Prior to amendment, last sentence read as follows: “To the extent that such distribution is treated as a distribution to which section 301 applies, the stock so acquired shall be treated as having been transferred by the person from whom acquired, and as having been received by the corporation acquiring it, as a contribution to the capital of such corporation.”Pub. (A) and (B) former subpar. Effective Date of 1987 Amendment, , , as amended by , (4), , , 3606, provided that:Pub. L. 100–203, title X, § 10223(d)101 Stat. 1330–412Pub. L. 100–647, title II, § 2004(k)(3)102 Stat. 3605Dec. 22, 1987Nov. 10, 1988“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 337 and 355 of this title] shall apply to distributions or transfers after .December 15, 1987“(2)Exceptions.—“(A).—DistributionsThe amendments made by this section shall not apply to any distribution after , and before , if—December 15, 1987January 1, 1993“(i)80 percent or more of the stock of the distributing corporation was acquired by the distributee before , orDecember 15, 1987“(ii)80 percent or more of the stock of the distributing corporation was acquired by the distributee before , pursuant to a binding written contract or tender offer in effect on .January 1, 1989December 15, 1987For purposes of the preceding sentence, stock described in section 1504(a)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall not be taken into account.“(B).—Section 304 transfersThe amendment made by subsection (c) [amending this section] shall not apply to any transfer after , and on or before , if such transfer is—December 15, 1987March 31, 1988“(i)between corporations which are members of the same affiliated group on , orDecember 15, 1987“(ii)between corporations which become members of the same affiliated group pursuant to a binding written contract or tender offer in effect on .December 15, 1987“(C).—Distributions covered by prior transition ruleThe amendments made by this section shall not apply to any distribution to which the amendments made by subtitle D of title VI of the Tax Reform Act of 1986 [sections 631 to 634 of , see Tables for classification] do not apply.Pub. L. 99–514“(D)Treatment of certain members of affiliated group.—“(i).—In generalFor purposes of subparagraph (A), all corporations which were in existence on the designated date and were members of the same affiliated group which included the distributees on such date shall be treated as 1 distributee.“(ii).—Limitation to stock held on designated dateClause (i) shall not exempt any distribution from the amendments made by this section if such distribution is with respect to stock not held by the distributee (determined without regard to clause (i)) on the designated date directly or indirectly through a corporation which goes out of existence in the transaction.“(iii).—Designated dateFor purposes of this subparagraph, the term ‘designated date’ means the later of—“(I), orDecember 15, 1987“(II)the date on which the acquisition meeting the requirements of subparagraph (A) occurred.”

children.heading: Treatment of certain stock purchases
children.label: a Treatment of certain stock purchases

children.heading: Acquisition by related corporation (other than subsidiary)
children.label: 1 Acquisition by related corporation (other than subsidiary)
A
children.text: one or more persons are in control of each of two corporations, and

children.label: B
children.text: in return for property, one of the corporations acquires stock in the other corporation from the person (or persons) so in control,

children.label: A
children.text: in return for property, one corporation acquires from a shareholder of another corporation stock in such other corporation, and

children.label: B
children.text: the issuing corporation controls the acquiring corporation,

children.heading: Special rules for application of subsection (a)
children.label: b Special rules for application of subsection (a)

children.heading: Rules for determinations under section 302(b)
children.label: 1 Rules for determinations under section 302(b)
children.text: In the case of any acquisition of stock to which subsection (a) of this section applies, determinations as to whether the acquisition is, by reason of section 302(b), to be treated as a distribution in part or full payment in exchange for the stock shall be made by reference to the stock of the issuing corporation. In applying section 318(a) (relating to constructive ownership of stock) with respect to section 302(b) for purposes of this paragraph, sections 318(a)(2)(C) and 318(a)(3)(C) shall be applied without regard to the 50 percent limitation contained therein.

children.label: A
children.text: by the acquiring corporation to the extent of its earnings and profits, and

children.label: B
children.text: then by the issuing corporation to the extent of its earnings and profits.

children.heading: Property treated as received in redemption
children.label: A Property treated as received in redemption
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, subsection (a) (and not section 351 and not so much of sections 357 and 358 as relates to section 351) shall apply to any property received in a distribution described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Certain assumptions of liability, etc.
children.label: B Certain assumptions of liability, etc.
I
children.text: assumed by the acquiring corporation, or

children.label: II
children.text: to which the stock is subject,

children.heading: Extension of obligations, etc.
children.label: ii Extension of obligations, etc.
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), an extension, renewal, or refinancing of a liability which meets the requirements of clause (i) shall be treated as meeting such requirements.

children.heading: Clause (i) does not apply to stock acquired from related person except where complete termination
children.label: iii Clause (i) does not apply to stock acquired from related person except where complete termination
I
children.text: none of whose stock is attributable to the transferor under section 318(a) (other than paragraph (4) thereof), or

children.label: II
children.text: who satisfies rules similar to the rules of section 302(c)(2) with respect to both the acquiring and the issuing corporations (determined as if such person were a distributee of each such corporation).

children.heading: Distributions incident to formation of bank holding companies
children.label: C Distributions incident to formation of bank holding companies
i
children.text: pursuant to a plan, control of a bank is acquired and within 2 years after the date on which such control is acquired, stock constituting control of such bank is transferred to a BHC in connection with its formation,

children.label: ii
children.text: incident to the formation of the BHC there is a distribution of property described in subsection (a), and

children.label: iii
children.text: the shareholders of the BHC who receive distributions of such property do not have control of such BHC,

children.heading: Qualified minority shareholder
children.label: i Qualified minority shareholder
children.text: The term “qualified minority shareholder” means any shareholder who owns less than 10 percent (in value) of the stock of the BHC. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the rules of paragraph (3) of subsection (c) shall apply.

children.heading: BHC
children.label: ii BHC
children.text: The term “BHC” means a bank holding company (within the meaning of section 2(a) of the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956).

children.label: i
children.text: the adjusted basis of any intragroup stock, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the earnings and profits of any member of such group,

children.heading: Affiliated group
children.label: i Affiliated group
children.text: The term “affiliated group” has the meaning given such term by section 1504(a).

children.label: I
children.text: is in a corporation which is a member of an affiliated group, and

children.label: II
children.text: is held by another member of such group.

children.label: I
children.text: a United States shareholder (within the meaning of section 951(b)) of the acquiring corporation, and

children.label: II
children.text: the transferor or a person who bears a relationship to the transferor described in section 267(b) or 707(b), and

children.label: ii
children.text: which were accumulated during the period or periods such stock was owned by such person while the acquiring corporation was a controlled foreign corporation.

children.heading: Special rule in case of foreign acquiring corporation
children.label: B Special rule in case of foreign acquiring corporation
i
children.text: be subject to tax under this chapter for the taxable year in which the dividends arise, nor

children.label: ii
children.text: be includible in the earnings and profits of a controlled foreign corporation (as defined in section 957 and without regard to section 953(c)).

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: C Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as are appropriate to carry out the purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Avoidance of multiple inclusions, etc.
children.label: 6 Avoidance of multiple inclusions, etc.
children.text: In the case of any acquisition to which subsection (a) applies in which the acquiring corporation or the issuing corporation is a foreign corporation, the Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as are appropriate in order to eliminate a multiple inclusion of any item in income by reason of this subpart and to provide appropriate basis adjustments (including modifications to the application of sections 959 and 961).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, control means the ownership of stock possessing at least 50 percent of the total combined voting power of all classes of stock entitled to vote, or at least 50 percent of the total value of shares of all classes of stock. If a person (or persons) is in control (within the meaning of the preceding sentence) of a corporation which in turn owns at least 50 percent of the total combined voting power of all stock entitled to vote of another corporation, or owns at least 50 percent of the total value of the shares of all classes of stock of another corporation, then such person (or persons) shall be treated as in control of such other corporation.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: A General rule
children.text: Where 1 or more persons in control of the issuing corporation transfer stock of such corporation in exchange for stock of the acquiring corporation, the stock of the acquiring corporation received shall be taken into account in determining whether such person or persons are in control of the acquiring corporation.

children.heading: Definition of control group
children.label: B Definition of control group
children.text: Where 2 or more persons in control of the issuing corporation transfer stock of such corporation to the acquiring corporation and, after the transfer, the transferors are in control of the acquiring corporation, the person or persons in control of each corporation shall include each of the persons who so transfer stock.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Section 318(a) (relating to constructive ownership of stock) shall apply for purposes of determining control under this section.

children.heading: Modification of 50-percent limitations in section 318
children.label: B Modification of 50-percent limitations in section 318
i
children.text: paragraph (2)(C) of section 318(a) shall be applied by substituting “5 percent” for “50 percent”, and

children.label: I
children.text: by substituting “5 percent” for “50 percent”, and

children.label: II
children.text: in any case where such paragraph would not apply but for subclause (I), by considering a corporation as owning the stock (other than stock in such corporation) owned by or for any shareholder of such corporation in that proportion which the value of the stock which such shareholder owned in such corporation bears to the value of all stock in such corporation.
